summary: First full test pass on Interio Junction CRM. 28/31 backend pytest tests pass (3 skipped due to state dependencies — no available stage-3 lead without completed measurement and no project-lead-not-assigned-to-sales after earlier tests). Fixed 5 backend serialization bugs (MongoDB ObjectId leak in POST responses) which were causing 500s on /api/users, /api/documents, /api/revisions, /api/payments, /api/measurements, /api/activities and POST /api/leads. Frontend Command Center, Lead Scoring, Automations and Pipeline pages render correctly with full design system (Fraunces/Hanken, bone/espresso/terracotta palette, ₹X L / ₹X Cr formatting, heat chips, stage dots, KPIs, weight sliders, gates, live signals).

minor.issue: Returned 500 due to MongoDB _id ObjectId leaking into JSON response after insert_one. FIXED in /app/backend/server.py by popping _id before return.

issues: Recharts emits repeated 'width(-1) height(-1)' warnings during first paint — non-blocking, container resolves; consider setting minHeight on ResponsiveContainer wrapper to silence.

action_items: Validate main agent: backend POST endpoints have been patched to strip Mongo _id (server.py edits, 7 spots). Hot reload picked up. Please review patches.

Consider adding a POST /api/auth/logout call (not just clearing localStorage) on UI logout to invalidate httpOnly cookies — current logout testid was not located so couldn't fully verify role-based UI restrictions in browser (admin verified visually).
Optional: set minHeight on Recharts ResponsiveContainer wrappers to remove width=-1 warnings on first render.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1220 lines — should be split into routers (auth, leads, pipeline, documents, scoring, automations, analytics).
MongoDB _id pollution pattern: every POST endpoint manually pops _id. Better fix is a small helper `clean(doc)` or use insert_one then re-fetch via find_one with projection {'_id':0}.

Designer/supervisor role authorization in /api/revisions and /api/measurements relies on user-provided project_id but never asserts the project is visible to that user — a designer can POST a revision for any project_id. Recommend ensuring proj visibility.
evaluate_gate Stage 5->6 uses sum of payment statuses but counts total = sum of ALL statuses including 'Pending' — math is correct, but worth a test for edge case 0 payments.
JWT_SECRET committed in backend/.env — for production should come from secret manager.
CORS_ORIGINS='*' with credentials in code falls back to allow_creds=False — browsers using withCredentials will fail in prod; needs explicit origin list.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (popped _id in 7 POST handlers: users, leads, measurements, revisions, payments, documents, activities)

success_rate.frontend: Admin UI verified visually for nav, Pipeline, Lead Scoring, Automations, Command Center

test_credentials: admin@interiojunction.com / interio2026 (+sales/designer/supervisor same password) — verified login works for all 4 via API; admin verified via UI.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_ prefixed user via POST /api/users; uploaded one test.txt document via POST /api/documents; multiple stage transitions executed on seeded leads — state of seeded data slightly mutated (some leads moved to higher stages).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend MongoDB _id leaks have been fixed at insert sites. Seed data state may have been mutated by stage-move tests (some leads now at later stages); re-run seeding if needed. Sales-unauthorized document download test skipped on this run because seeded data left no project-lead unowned by sales — consider creating a fresh isolated test lead. Cookie-only logout API not invoked by test script — couldn't traverse multi-role nav UI in one Playwright session; backend RBAC tests cover the role boundaries.
rca of the issue: Root cause for the 500s: motor's insert_one mutates the passed dict by injecting a BSON ObjectId into the '_id' key. Endpoints returned that mutated dict directly; FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId and raised ValueError(['ObjectId is not iterable', 'vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute']). Reproduction: POST /api/users {email,role,full_name} -> 500. Mitigation applied: doc.pop('_id', None) before returning in all create handlers. Long-term: use a serialization helper or projection re-fetch.
